move_account.title: How do I move my account to a new device?
move_account.desc: Since NYX stores your keys and history locally for maximum privacy, you cannot simply log in to see old messages. You have two secure ways to move:
move_account.migration_text: Go to <1>Settings > Transfer to New Device</1>. This creates a secure, direct tunnel to your new phone via QR code.
move_account.backup_text: Go to <1>Settings > Export Vault</1>. Save the `.nyxvault` file and import it on your new device login screen.

sandbox_mode.title: Why is my account "Sandboxed"?
sandbox_mode.desc: To prevent spam bots without collecting personal data (like phone numbers), new accounts start in <1>Sandbox Mode</1> with limited messaging quotas.
sandbox_mode.upgrade_text: You can upgrade to <1>VIP Status</1> instantly for free by verifying you are human. Go to <2>Settings > Upgrade to VIP</2> and choose either <3>Biometric Verification</3> (Fingerprint/FaceID) or <3>Proof of Work</3> (CPU Mining).

forgot_password.title: I forgot my password. What do I do?
forgot_password.desc: Because we don't have your email, we cannot send you a reset link. You must use your <1>Recovery Phrase</1>.
forgot_password.step1: On the login screen, click "Restore from phrase".
forgot_password.step2: Enter your 24-word Recovery Phrase to prove your identity.
forgot_password.warning: Note: Restoring from a phrase resets your identity keys. You will regain access to your account ID, but <1>chat history will be lost</1> unless you have a Vault Backup.

sync_issues.title: Why are my messages not syncing?
sync_issues.desc: NYX is <1>Local-First</1>. Messages live on your device, not the cloud. If you use NYX on multiple devices (e.g. Phone + Laptop), they act as independent clients.
sync_issues.future_text: We are working on a secure "Sync Protocol" for the future, but currently, history does not automatically sync between devices to ensure zero-knowledge privacy.

lost_phrase.title: What if I lose my Recovery Phrase?
lost_phrase.desc: We do not store your phrase or password. If you lose them, no one on Earth—including us—can decrypt your data. Please write down your phrase and store it safely offline.
